preamble: The lateral affricate [tɬ] can lenite to the voiceless alveolar lateral fricative [ɬ]. This shift reduces the stop component of the affricate while preserving the lateral frication.

examples.note: Lenition of affricates is common in rapid speech or specific phonological contexts.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Lastra de Suárez, Y. (1986). Las áreas dialectales del náhuatl moderno.
